rationale: The citing paper uses Deng et al. (2016) as a meaningful prior anchor in framing its contribution, not just as a background list citation. In both the introduction and General Discussion, it explicitly positions its findings relative to Deng et al.'s display-orientation effects, arguing that similar downstream outcomes can be produced by changing spatial arrangement without changing display orientation. This comparative positioning helps define the citing paper's novelty and theoretical contribution about disfluency, exploratory search, and unfamiliar choice.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Deng et al. (2016) by shifting from horizontal-versus-vertical orientation to systematic-versus-unsystematic arrangement and showing related perceptual-disfluency and choice consequences. It presents its results as conceptually consistent with, but broader than, orientation-based display effects.
